item.prompt:
You are the planner for a lean team bug-fix task.
Task: In this app, the player and volume colors on the custom theme should follow the same color as the icons. Also, the edited page crashes after running for a while, likely due to a memory leak. Build type: bug fix. Quality bar: high. Keep changes minimal.
Deliver a short execution plan with: likely investigation areas/files, probable root causes, suggested implementation packets with disjoint file ownership if possible, and focused validation steps. Do not edit code.

Plan and ownership are set now, and the work can safely split in parallel.

1. Engineer A owns `popup/popup.js` and `popup/popup.css`.
   Goal: make the custom-theme preview use the same accent/color token as the icon color, especially for the volume UI and any theme-selected state in the popup.

2. Engineer B owns `content/audible.js`.
   Goal: remove hardcoded accent drift in the injected player/volume UI and reduce the long-running observer churn that is likely causing the crash.

After both packets land, the tester will run focused checks on custom-theme color propagation and long-session stability, then the reviewer will do a final regression pass.

You are Engineer 1. File ownership: /Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js only. Do not edit any other file. You are not alone in the codebase; do not revert others' changes, and accommodate concurrent work.
Task: implement a minimal high-quality fix for two issues in the content script: (1) on custom theme, player controls and volume UI should follow the same color as the icons; (2) the edited page crashes after running for a while, likely from a media/audio lifecycle leak. Investigate and patch content/audible.js only. Run focused local validation if possible. In your final message, summarize the root cause, the exact changes, validation, and list changed files.

data.text:
**Plan**
1. Sync custom-theme accent tokens so popup controls and injected player/volume UI use the same icon color.
2. Reduce long-running content-script churn in the webplayer to address the crash/leak behavior.
3. Run focused validation, then do a final regression review.

**Execution**
- Planner phase isolated the work into two non-overlapping packets: popup preview styling and webplayer/content-script behavior.
- Engineer A updated [popup/popup.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.js#L26) and [popup/popup.css](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.css#L229) so the popup’s selected theme state and volume UI inherit the custom icon color instead of fixed orange tokens.
- Engineer B updated [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2058) and [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2395) so custom theme accent/copy values propagate into the speed/player controls and volume widget, and tightened the mutation path in [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L1897) and [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2605) by throttling icon refreshes, ignoring extension-injected nodes, dropping `style` attribute observation, and keeping observer lifecycle explicit.
- Tester checks passed for syntax and manifest integrity. Reviewer pass found no blocking issues in the diff; the remaining gap is runtime soak validation in a real Audible session.

**Summary**
Changed files:
- [popup/popup.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.js)
- [popup/popup.css](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.css)
- [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js)

Tests run:
- `node --check popup/popup.js`
- `node --check content/audible.js`
- `node -e 'JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync("manifest.json","utf8"))'`

Unresolved risks:
- No live browser soak test was run, so the observer/memory-leak fix is validated statically, not against a long-running Audible webplayer session.

Follow-up:
- Manually run the extension on `www.audible.<tld>/webplayer`, switch to `custom`, verify speed/player and volume accents match the icon color, then leave the page running long enough to confirm the crash no longer reproduces.

data.text:
**Plan**
1. Unify the custom-theme accent path so player UI and volume controls use the same token as the icon set.
2. Fix the long-lived media lifecycle so removed webplayer elements do not keep audio graph state alive.
3. Run focused static validation and a regression review.

**Execution**
Planner pass identified a single-file fix in [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js). Engineer ownership stayed there only; existing popup worktree changes were left untouched.

Engineer work in [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2059) and [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2365) now derives speed-popover and volume-widget colors from shared theme accent tokens, and custom mode explicitly sets `--audible-tools-accent` from the icon/text color in [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2416). The stability fix in [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2473) stores/disconnects full media-node connections, defers disconnects for temporarily re-parented media in [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2505), and narrows observer churn plus teardown behavior in [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2597) and [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2642). The icon refresh path was also throttled in [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L1898) to reduce repeated full-page rescans.

Tester checks passed: `node --check content/audible.js` and `git diff --check -- content/audible.js`. Reviewer pass found no blocking issues in the changed path; main residual risk is that I could not run a live Audible browser soak test from this sandbox.

**Summary**
Changed files: [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js)

Tests run:
- `node --check content/audible.js`
- `git diff --check -- content/audible.js`

Unresolved risks:
- No live in-browser verification against Audible’s production DOM churn.
- Concurrent local changes already existed in `popup/popup.js` and `popup/popup.css`; they were not modified as part of this task.

Follow-up work:
- Manual 10-15 minute webplayer soak test with `custom` theme enabled, changing chapters/menu states and leaving playback running to confirm memory stays stable and volume/speed controls match icon color.

data.text:
No hard failures from the lightweight validation pass.

Checks run:
- `node --check popup/popup.js`
- `node --check content/audible.js`
- `git diff --check -- popup/popup.js popup/popup.css content/audible.js`
- `node -e 'JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync("manifest.json","utf8"))'`

Evidence:
- Both JS files parse cleanly: [popup/popup.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.js#L1) and [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L1).
- Diff hygiene is clean for the three target files.
- `manifest.json` is valid JSON.
- Static inspection matches the intended fix:
  - Popup theme tokens now derive from the selected/custom accent in [popup/popup.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.js#L66) and are consumed by the selected theme chip / volume styles in [popup/popup.css](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.css#L229) and [popup/popup.css](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.css#L323).
  - Webplayer speed + volume controls now consume shared accent tokens in [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2059) and [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2366).
  - Media lifecycle handling now stores reconnectable media graph state and narrows observer churn in [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2473) and [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2642).

Remaining gaps / risks:
- I could not run a real browser soak test, so the leak fix is only statically validated.
- The highest-risk assumption is the teardown path in [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2635): `stopMediaObserver()` schedules disconnects through `cleanupMediaInTree(document)`, but those timers no-op if media is still connected when they fire. If Audible does a same-document route transition and removes media after the observer is already stopped, that cleanup may be missed. I did not prove this is broken, but it is the main remaining static concern.

Targeted manual reproduction:
1. Load the extension unpacked and open `www.audible.<tld>/webplayer`.
2. Set `Custom` theme and choose an obvious non-orange copy/icon color, for example `#00c2ff`.
3. Verify the popup selected-theme chip, popup volume slider, webplayer speed popover, and injected volume widget all use that same accent.
4. Leave playback running for 10-15 minutes while opening/closing chapters, bookmarks, drawer, and speed controls.
5. In Chrome DevTools Performance Monitor or Memory panel, watch JS heap, DOM nodes, and event listeners for steady growth.

data.text:
**Plan**
1. Align the custom-theme accent path so popup controls, player controls, and volume UI all read from the same icon color source.
2. Reduce long-running content-script churn and clean up media-node lifecycle so the webplayer does not accumulate stale audio graph state.
3. Validate with focused static checks and call out the remaining runtime risk.

**Execution**
The final workspace now propagates the custom theme accent through the popup and the webplayer UI. In [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js), the player/speed/volume controls now derive their accent from shared theme tokens, custom mode explicitly sets `--audible-tools-accent` from the icon color, media connections keep full source/gain state so they can be disconnected safely, and the mutation observer was narrowed to reduce unnecessary rescans. I also added an observer restart in `applySettings()` so the watcher comes back if the page state changes without a full reload.

The popup changes already present in the workspace are consistent with that direction: [popup/popup.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.js) now computes shared accent variables from the selected theme, and [popup/popup.css](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.css) uses those variables for the selected theme state and volume slider.

**Summary**
Changed files:
- [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js)
- [popup/popup.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.js)
- [popup/popup.css](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.css)

Tests run:
- `node --check content/audible.js`
- `node --check popup/popup.js`
- `node -e 'JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync("manifest.json","utf8"))'`
- `git diff --check -- content/audible.js popup/popup.js popup/popup.css`

Unresolved risks:
- No live browser soak test was possible here, so the memory-leak/crash fix is statically validated, not confirmed against a long-running Audible session.
- The dedicated tester/reviewer threads were started, but their reports did not return before close; the final gate here is my local validation and code review, which found no blocking issues.

Follow-up work:
- Run the extension on `www.audible.<tld>/webplayer`, switch to `custom`, verify player/volume/speed accents match the icon color, and leave playback running for 10-15 minutes to confirm the page no longer degrades or crashes.

data.text:
No new static issue from the follow-up patch.

Checks rerun:
- `node --check content/audible.js`
- `git diff --check -- popup/popup.js popup/popup.css content/audible.js`
- `node -e 'JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync("manifest.json","utf8"))'`

Evidence:
- The new immediate teardown path is present in [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2606) and [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2643).
- `cleanupMediaInTree(root, { immediate = false } = {})` now supports both deferred and immediate disconnect modes, and `stopMediaObserver()` now uses `cleanupMediaInTree(document, { immediate: true })`, which closes the earlier static concern about missed cleanup after the observer is stopped.
- JS syntax, diff hygiene, and manifest validity still pass.

Failures:
- None from the lightweight checks.

Remaining gaps:
- Prior validations still stand.
- The only meaningful remaining gap is unchanged: no live browser soak test against the Audible webplayer, so the leak/crash fix is still statically validated rather than runtime-proven.

data.text:
No blocking findings in this delta.

The change in [content/audible.js#L2606](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2606) and [content/audible.js#L2643](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2643) looks like the right correction. Using `immediate: true` in `stopMediaObserver()` removes the remaining gap where teardown could leave zero-delay disconnects queued after the observer was already shut down. The updated `cleanupMediaInTree()` keeps the prior “defer on transient DOM removal” behavior for mutation handling, while making explicit shutdown deterministic.

Residual risk is unchanged from the previous review: this still needs a real browser soak check because the correctness question is behavioral, not syntactic. The main thing to watch manually is SPA-style navigation away from and back to `/webplayer`: volume boost and playback speed should still reattach cleanly after an immediate disconnect.

data.text:
**Plan**
1. Unify the custom-theme accent path so popup controls, player controls, and volume UI use the same icon color source.
2. Reduce long-running webplayer churn and clean up media-node lifecycle to address the crash/leak behavior.
3. Validate with tester and reviewer before closing.

**Execution**
- Planner split the work into popup theme propagation and webplayer/content-script stability.
- Popup theme tokens now derive from the selected/custom accent in [popup/popup.js#L66](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.js#L66), and the selected theme chip plus popup volume slider now consume those tokens in [popup/popup.css#L229](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.css#L229) and [popup/popup.css#L323](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.css#L323).
- Webplayer speed and volume UI now read from shared theme accent tokens in [content/audible.js#L2059](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2059) and [content/audible.js#L2365](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2365), and custom mode explicitly maps the icon/text color into `--audible-tools-accent` in [content/audible.js#L2416](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2416).
- The stability fix now throttles icon rescans in [content/audible.js#L1898](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L1898), keeps reconnectable media graph state in [content/audible.js#L2473](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2473), defers cleanup for transient removals but performs immediate teardown when the observer stops in [content/audible.js#L2606](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2606) and [content/audible.js#L2643](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2643), and restarts the observer from settings application in [content/audible.js#L2736](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js#L2736).
- Tester and reviewer both came back with no blocking findings.

**Summary**
Changed files:
- [content/audible.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/content/audible.js)
- [popup/popup.js](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.js)
- [popup/popup.css](/Users/eduardoverona/hackaton/audible-tools/popup/popup.css)

Tests run:
- `node --check popup/popup.js`
- `node --check content/audible.js`
- `git diff --check -- popup/popup.js popup/popup.css content/audible.js`
- `node -e 'JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync("manifest.json","utf8"))'`

Unresolved risks:
- No live browser soak test was possible here, so the leak fix is statically validated, not runtime-proven against a long Audible session.

Follow-up:
- Run a 10-15 minute manual soak on `www.audible.<tld>/webplayer` with `Custom` theme enabled and verify the popup, speed popover, and injected volume control all match the icon color while heap/listener counts stay stable.
